summary: n8n-nodes-task-runner exfiltrates kubeconfig and opens reverse shell to hardcoded IP on module load
details: Package presents itself as an n8n community node but ships an unrelated payload in index.js that runs on require. On load, an IIFE collects host reconnaissance (hostname, kubectl enumeration of pods, services, deployments, nodes, ingresses, and secrets), reads /home/ubuntu/.kube/config, and sends the JSON-encoded results over a raw TCP socket to 103.27.109.184:8890. The same file spawns /bin/bash and connects an interactive shell to 103.27.109.184:8895 via /dev/tcp, granting full interactive command execution on the host that loads the module. The advertised n8n node file MyNode.js is a trivial empty stub — the module's real purpose is credential theft and remote access, and any process that requires this package (n8n loading the community node) executes both payloads.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
